data.title: Driven Tech Inc. honored as a 2025 CRN Triple Crown Award Winner by The Channel Company for excellence in solutions.
data.description: In a recent announcement on October 16, 2025, Driven Tech Inc., also known as Driven Technologies, revealed that they have been named a recipient of the prestigious 2025 Triple Crown Award by CRN, a subsidiary of The Channel Company. This distinguished accolade is given to solution providers who have been recognized on the following platforms.

data.title: Natural Nine enhances presence in personal care market with research-driven innovation in hygiene, targeting global appeal.
data.description: Hwaseong, South Korea - Natural Nine is making strides in research-driven innovation for personal care and hygiene products catered to both men and women worldwide. The company focuses on developing antibacterial solutions to address the needs of consumers in various markets.

data.title: Cryptos like Bitcoin & Ethereum slide, need close above $117,000. Bitcoin at $109,036.62, Ethereum $3,940.21. Liquidations hit $737 million in last 24 hours. Traders uncertain.
data.description: Leading cryptocurrencies, including Bitcoin and Ethereum, experienced declines on Thursday, with Bitcoin dropping to $107,500, its lowest in over three months. Ethereum hit an intraday low of $3,829.65 before recovering to $3,940. Selling pressure was evident as Bitcoin and Ethereum's trading volumes dropped 22% and 5% respectively in the last 24 hours. Liquidations amounted to $737 million, wiping out over $530 million in bullish long positions. Despite the downturn, Bitcoin's open interest rose by 2.85% to $73 billion, suggesting traders may be opening new short positions. Gold, in contrast, reached new highs. The full article is available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Fed's Waller suggests 5 more rate cuts needed to reach neutral policy stance, citing disinflation and labor softness.
data.description: Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller suggested the central bank may need to reduce rates by up to 125 basis points to achieve a more neutral policy stance due to signs of disinflation and weakening labor demand. Waller mentioned during a Bloomberg Council on Foreign Relations event that approximately five additional rate cuts could be necessary to reach the neutral rate, a level where the economy operates at full employment and stable inflation. Waller referred to the Summary of Economic Projections, indicating that the median rate is around 3%, leaving a gap of 125 basis points for the committee to reach neutrality if economic conditions move closer to the target. Further details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Bitfarms upsizes to US$500 million in convertible notes offering, with an option to purchase an additional $88 million.
data.description: Bitfarms Ltd., a North American company focusing on energy and digital infrastructure, has revealed the pricing of its offering of US$500 million aggregate principal amount of 1.375% convertible senior notes due 2031. The company granted initial purchasers of the Convertible Notes an option to acquire up to an additional US$88 million aggregate principal amount of the notes, within a 13-day window. Initially intended as a $300 million issuance (or $360 million if the initial purchasers fully exercise their option), the total offering size was raised. The offering, expected to close around October 21, 2025, is subject to customary closing conditions, including TSX approval.
